operation: Start with an XY workplane, draw a 30 x 20 unit rectangle, then extrude the sketch 8 units along the positive Z axis to form a rectangular base. Next, select the topmost Z face of the base, create a workplane on this face, sketch a centered ellipse with 12 unit and 8 unit semi-axis lengths, and perform a 4 unit deep blind cut in the negative Z direction to form an elliptical recess. Finally, select the second highest Z face (the flat floor of the elliptical recess), create a workplane on this face, and add a centered countersunk through hole with 5.0 unit hole diameter, 3.0 degree countersink angle, and 8.0 unit countersink diameter that passes fully through the remaining thickness of the base to the bottom face.
geometry: The model is a rectangular prism base, with a shallow elliptical recess centered on its upper surface. A countersunk through hole sits at the center of the elliptical recess, extending all the way through the base to its bottom face. All cut features are concentrically aligned to the central vertical axis of the rectangular base, and the elliptical recess only penetrates the upper half of the base's total thickness.
function: This is an aligned mounting base designed to seat a mating elliptical component in the top recess, with the countersunk through hole allowing flush installation of a fastener to secure the base to a mounting surface or attach the seated component to the base.
